The '''''Kyramud''-type battleship''', named after the [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]] word for ''killer'' or ''assassin'', comprised a series of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] used by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mandalorian Crusaders|Crusaders]]<ref name="TOTJTSW1" /> and [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusaders]].<ref name="SWKOTOR8" />

==Characteristics==
The ''Kyramud''-type battleship resembled the Basilisk war droids in aesthetic design, and were based on stolen [[Basiliskan]] design.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Later models, although similar to the type used during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], were sleeker designs, dispensing with the more organic features of the older model. They mounted armored "fins" along the sides.<ref name="SWKOTOR8" />

==History==
[[File:Kyramud-BattleOfVanquo-KotOR08.jpg|thumb|left|150px|''Kyramud''-type battleships at the [[Battle of Vanquo]].]]
These vessels were often used for ground assaults.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

At least one such ship attacked the [[Tetan carbonite smelter]] over [[Empress Teta/Legends|Empress Teta]], launching numerous open-air [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]].<ref name="TOTJTSW1" />
Different models were used by the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] in [[3964 BBY]], during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. These also carried Basilisk war droids as well as fighters.<ref name="SWKOTOR8" />

Mandalorian battleships were often accompanied by smaller warships and support ships when in battle. Before and during the Great Sith War, they were escorted by small insect-like vessels,<ref name="TOTJTSW1" /> while during the Mandalorian Wars, they were supported by [[Jehavey'ir-type assault ship|''Jehavey'ir''-type assault ships]] and [[Lictor-class dungeon ship|''Lictor''-class dungeon ships]].<ref name="SWKOTOR8" />

[[Rohlan Dyre]] served aboard a battleship named ''[[Jai'galaar]]'' prior to [[3963 BBY]] leading a squad of shocktroopers.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

*''[[Jai'galaar]]''
